ES1! · CME · RTH UPDATE FOR TUE JUN 23, 2026 · BUILT 06:05 ET PRE-OPEN

ES RTH Plan — Tue Jun 23 (Overnight Yield/AI Flush)

Single-plan RTH update · re-anchored to the overnight selloff · ref 7,436.25 (ON low 7,421.75) · Catalyst: 10-yr yield to ~4.51% & 2-yr at a 16-month high after the hawkish Warsh Fed; Alphabet/Amazon AI-capex selloff extended overnight; SpaceX/SPCX −16% · Brent ~$73 (US–Iran 60-day roadmap) · Tue calendar: flash PMIs, Richmond Fed, ADP, 2-yr auction · PCE Thu, Micron Wed
Ref 7,436.25 (ON 04:09) · ON low 7,421.75
Yest (Jun 22) RTH: O 7,573.25 · H 7,599.25 · L 7,527.25 · C 7,540.25
ON VAH 7,549.50 · ON POC 7,533.00 · ON VAL 7,449.00
vs 20-EMA 7,515.50 −79 (LOST)
vs 50-EMA 7,399.25 +37 (next support)
VIX 19.45 ▲5.4% (waking up)
ATR 110.25 (expanding)
▼ OVERNIGHT UPDATE — the 7,514 shelf broke exactly as flagged; this is now a trend-day-down context, not a dip-buy Yesterday's RTH plan's key bear trigger was "a 30-min close below 7,514 on a yields/AI headline re-opens 7,496 → 7,463" — that is precisely what happened. Overnight, rising Treasury yields (10-yr ~4.51%, 2-yr at a 16-month high after the hawkish Warsh Fed) plus an extension of the Alphabet/Amazon AI-capex selloff drove ES straight through the 7,514 single print, the 7,496 spike base, and the 7,463 prev VAL down to an ON low of 7,421.75 — a ~120-pt overnight decline. Price now trades 7,436.25, having bounced off the 7,421/7,409 demand shelf, but it is below the daily 20-EMA (7,515.50) for the first time since the April repair and below all of yesterday's value. The overnight session built its own distribution: ON VAH 7,549.50 / ON POC 7,533.00 / ON VAL 7,449.00, leaving downside single prints at 7,493.50 / 7,479.25 / 7,466.25 (speed zones that will fill fast on any bounce attempt) and an unfilled gap of supply back up to yesterday's value. Regime read: this is no longer a megacap-only rotation — it has broadened into a yields-driven repricing (VIX finally responding, +5.4% to 19.45; ATR expanded to 110.25). The dip-buy template from yesterday is suspended until price reclaims the 7,449 ON VAL / 7,463 prev VAL band. The structure now favors selling rallies into the 7,449–7,466 single-print band and treating 7,399 (50-EMA) / 7,357 (prev POC) as the downside objectives. A reclaim of 7,463–7,469 flips it back to two-sided. No fresh MOC (pre-open); event risk is the 2-yr auction + Thursday PCE.

01Top-Down Analysis — Tue Jun 23 RTH (pre-open)

Reference 7,436.25 after a ~120-pt overnight flush. Price is below the daily 20-EMA and below all of yesterday's value; the session question is whether the 7,421/7,409 demand shelf holds into the cash open or whether the 50-EMA (7,399) / prev POC (7,357) come into play. This is a sell-rallies structure until 7,463 is reclaimed.

a) Daily — MA stack & drawn levels

Daily ES1! — price 7,434 BELOW the 20-EMA 7,515.50 for the first time since the April repair; 50-EMA 7,399.25 is the next support; full stack still rising · ATR 110.25 · ATH 7,694.75
MA / LevelValueDistanceStatus
Spot (ON)7,436.25—Below 20-EMA, above 50-EMA
20-EMA7,515.50−79.25LOST first close below since April repair
50-EMA7,399.25+37.00next support — the line that defines the uptrend
100-EMA7,243.75+192.50rising
200-EMA7,043.00+393.25rising
300-EMA6,876.50+559.75rising
ATR (daily)110.25—Expanded from 102.50 — volatility waking up
ATH7,694.75+258.50Record; now 3.4% away
Why the 20-EMA loss mattersThe 20-EMA (7,515.50) had been the buy-the-dip line for the entire April–June advance — every pullback held it on a closing basis. Overnight price sliced ~79 pts through it without a pause, which tells you the order flow that defended it is gone: the responsive buyers who showed up at the 20-EMA all spring stepped aside under the yields/AI pressure. That converts the 20-EMA from support into overhead resistance — the first place trapped longs will sell to get out. The 50-EMA at 7,399 is now the structural line: hold it on a closing basis and the broader uptrend is a deep-but-intact pullback; lose it and the character shifts from "pullback" to "trend reversal attempt," opening the 7,357 prev POC and the 7,300s.

b) 30-min — session flow & drawn levels

SessionH / L / CCharacter & value migration

Jun 19—Juneteenth — closed
Jun 22 RTH7,599 / 7,527 / 7,540Distribution down — sold the 7,599 weak high, value lower, closed on POC
Jun 22→23 ON~7,549 / 7,421 / 7,436Trend-down flush — broke 7,514/7,496/7,463, built ON value 7,549/7,533/7,449
Now (pre-open)7,436.25Bouncing off the 7,421/7,409 shelf, below all yesterday value

fib ext down7,350.00red−86Lower measured target
spike base7,258.25teal−178Deep structural base
Why the 7,449–7,466 band is the controlling resistanceThree broken levels stack here in a 17-pt band: the ON VAL 7,449, the prev RTH VAL 7,463.50, and the ON single prints 7,466.25 / 7,479.25 / 7,493.50. When price breaks down through value and then bounces, the old value-low becomes the first place sellers re-engage — they're defending the level that just gave way. The single prints inside this band are speed zones: thin auction created on the way down, so a bounce will travel up through them quickly, but they offer no support on the way back down. That makes 7,449–7,466 a high-quality sell-the-rally zone — price is likely to be pulled up into it (to fill the thin prints and test the broken value) and then rejected, unless buyers can reclaim 7,463 on a closing basis and refill the prints.
Why 7,421/7,409 is the shelf that matters on the downsideThe overnight low 7,421.75 sits right on the prev RTH VAH 7,409.50 + single print 7,403.50 shelf — an old value-area edge that flipped to support. It held the first overnight test. If it holds again at the cash open, it's a responsive-buy base for a bounce into the 7,449–7,466 sell zone. If it breaks, the 50-EMA 7,399 + fib ext 7,396.50 cluster is the last trend-defining support before the 7,357 prev POC opens up — that break is what turns "deep pullback" into "trend reversal."

c) Profile — exact data & structure

MetricJun 18 (Prev)Jun 22 (Yest)

High7,581.507,599.25
Low7,535.507,527.25
Close7,564.257,540.25

VAL7,549.007,527.25

Close LocInside VA > POCInside VA > POC
VA ShiftOverlap LowerOverlap Lower

Overnight profile (the actionable one): ON VAH 7,549.50 / ON POC 7,533.00 / ON VAL 7,449.00 — a long, thin, downward distribution (the opposite of balance) with the bulk of time spent up near 7,533 before the flush. The downside single prints 7,493.50 / 7,479.25 / 7,466.25 mark where the auction moved too fast to build value — these are the vacuum the cash session will either refill (bounce) or confirm (continuation).

STRUCTURE MAP (ref 7,436.25) 7,599.25 ── yest weak high / ON high ▲ heavy supply (old value above) 7,549.50 ── ON VAH 7,533.00 ── ON POC 7,514.25 ── broken shelf 7,496.25 ── broken spike base 7,466–7,493 ── ON SINGLE PRINTS (speed zones / sell band) 7,463.50 ── broken prev VAL 7,449.00 ── ON VAL ◄ FIRST RESISTANCE 7,442.50 ── buying tail 7,436.25 ●● SPOT 7,421.75 ── ON low ◄ first support 7,409.50 ── prev VAH (demand shelf) 7,399.25 ── 50-EMA ◄ trend line 7,396.50 ── fib ext 7,357.75 ── prev POC ▼ downside objectives 7,258.25 ── deep spike base

d) VIX & macro

VIX 19.45, up 5.4% — finally responding after sitting flat through Monday's down day. That's the tell that this move is different in character: Monday was orderly rotation (VIX flat); the overnight break has fear starting to bid. Still sub-20, so not a panic — but the "orderly, lean-less-bearish" read from yesterday no longer applies while VIX is rising. Macro: the driver has shifted from megacap-only to a yields-led repricing — 10-yr ~4.51%, 2-yr at a 16-month high after the hawkish Warsh Fed, with the AI-capex selloff (Alphabet, Amazon) compounding it. Brent ~$73 (Iran 60-day roadmap) is a disinflationary offset but isn't moving equities. Tuesday event risk: flash PMIs, Richmond Fed, ADP, and a 2-yr note auction (yield-sensitive); then Micron Wed and PCE Thu — the auction and PCE are the binary catalysts that decide whether 7,399 holds.

🔗 Controlling clusters7,449–7,466 (ON VAL + broken prev VAL + ON single prints) is the dense sell-the-rally band — the single most important resistance for the session. Below, 7,396–7,409 (prev VAH demand shelf + 50-EMA + fib ext) is the make-or-break support cluster: hold = bounce/range, lose = trend continuation to 7,357. Spot sits in a 13-pt no-man's-land between them; the edges are where the trades are.

03Context Summary — RTH

TPO + overnight profile — ON VAH 7,549.50 / ON POC 7,533.00 / ON VAL 7,449.00; ON single prints 7,493.50 / 7,479.25 / 7,466.25 (downside speed zones); price broke below ON VAL to 7,421.75 low
FactorRead
Prior day type (Jun 22)Distribution down, then an overnight trend-down flush
Settlement → ONClosed 7,540 on POC; overnight broke to 7,421.75 (−119 from close)
ON profile shapeLong thin downward distribution (not balance); ON POC 7,533 high in the range
Value migrationSharply LOWER — 3rd consecutive lower-value session
Structural featuresON single prints 7,493/7,479/7,466 (speed zones) · broken spike base 7,496 · broken prev VAL 7,463
Price vs valueBelow ON VAL, below all of yesterday's value — clean breakdown
Distance from old POC−97 from yest POC 7,538 — extended; snap-back rally risk into 7,449–7,466 exists
Daily MABelow 20-EMA (lost); 50-EMA 7,399 is the next/trend support
ATR / range110.25 (expanded); ON range already ~128 pts — volatility regime up
CatalystYields-led (10-yr 4.51%, 2-yr 16-mo high) + AI-capex selloff; VIX +5.4%
Event risk2-yr auction + PMIs/ADP today · Micron Wed · PCE Thu
MOCN/A — pre-open build
Why "extended from value" cuts both ways herePrice is 97 pts below yesterday's POC, which in a normal pullback would scream mean-reversion-long. But the extension was built on a genuine catalyst shift (yields breaking out, VIX waking up), not noise — so it's not an automatic snap-back. The resolution: extension creates a bounce (to refill the thin single prints up to 7,449–7,466), but the catalyst caps that bounce as a sell rather than a reversal. You play the snap-back as a fade target, not as a trend-long, until 7,463 is reclaimed.

04What-If Scenarios — RTH (Tue Jun 23)

Six paths keyed to the open vs the 7,449–7,466 sell band and the 7,399–7,409 support cluster. Each probability is defended against the evidence below.
G1 · Bounce-to-sell into 7,449–7,466SHORT★★★★★ 32%
Zoneopen 7,420–7,440 → rally to 7,449–7,466
NarrativeThe cash open holds the 7,421/7,409 shelf and bounces to refill the overnight single prints, tagging the broken ON VAL / prev VAL band — where trapped longs and fresh sellers re-engage and reject it back toward the lows.
SetupShort 7,449–7,463 on a rejection wick, stop 7,470, targets 7,422 / 7,409 / 7,399
Confirm / InvalidConfirm: first-tag rejection at the band + VIX stays bid · Invalid: 30-min close > 7,466
Why this probability★★★★★ 32% — value migrated lower 3 sessions, price is below a lost 20-EMA, VIX is rising (+5.4%), and the catalyst (yields) is still active. Broken-value-edge rallies failing is the textbook continuation behavior; this is the highest-conviction path.
G2 · Shelf-break trend continuation → 7,399/7,357SHORT★★★★ 24%
Zone7,421 → 7,399 → 7,357
NarrativeNo bounce — the open presses the 7,421 ON low, breaks the 7,409 shelf, and the 50-EMA/fib cluster 7,396–7,399 fails on a yield-auction or PCE-positioning impulse, opening the 7,357 prev POC.
SetupShort 7,408 on a confirmed break of 7,421, stop 7,425, targets 7,399 / 7,357; or wait and BUY 7,357–7,360
Confirm / InvalidConfirm: 30-min close < 7,409 · Invalid: reclaim 7,422 / 50-EMA holds on a wick
Why this probability★★★★ 24% — the trend-down momentum and expanded ATR (110) support follow-through, and the 2-yr auction is a live downside catalyst. Capped below G1 because the 7,396–7,409 cluster is a strong triple-support and the first test of a major MA usually holds.
G3 · Shelf-hold reversal-up → reclaim 7,463LONG★★★ 18%
Zone7,409–7,421 → 7,449 → 7,463
NarrativeResponsive buyers defend the 7,421/7,409 shelf and the 50-EMA, the 2-yr auction goes well / yields ease, and price reclaims the 7,463 prev VAL on a closing basis — flipping the broken band back to support and refilling the single prints toward 7,496.
SetupLong 7,410–7,422 on a wick + reclaim, stop 7,399, targets 7,449 / 7,463 / 7,496
Confirm / InvalidConfirm: 30-min close > 7,463 · Invalid: rejection back < 7,440
Why this probability★★★ 18% — the broader MA stack is still rising and the 50-EMA is a legitimate first-test buy; net-buy demand showed up at Monday's close (institutions were buying the dip). But it's a counter-trend long into a rising-VIX/active-catalyst tape, so it's third, not the base case.
G4 · Open-drive-down (gap-and-go)SHORT★★ 12%
Zone7,421 → 7,357
NarrativeA hot PMI/ADP or weak auction at/after the open drives straight down through the shelf and 50-EMA with no bounce — open-drive-down trend day.
SetupShort the open-drive only on a failed retest of 7,421 from below, stop 7,430, target 7,399 / 7,357
Confirm / InvalidConfirm: opens < 7,421 and doesn't reclaim it in 30 min · Invalid: reclaim 7,435
Why this probability★★ 12% — possible given the catalyst, but pure open-drive-down days off an already-extended overnight low are less common than a bounce-first path; the market usually retraces the thin prints before continuing.
G5 · Big snap-back to old value (7,533)LONG★★ 9%
Zone7,463 → 7,496 → 7,533
NarrativeYields reverse (auction stops through / dovish surprise) and the whole overnight move is a stop-run; price reclaims 7,463, fills every single print, and mean-reverts to the ON/yest POC 7,533.
SetupLong only after a confirmed 7,463 reclaim, stop 7,449, targets 7,496 / 7,514 / 7,533
Confirm / InvalidConfirm: 30-min accept > 7,496 · Invalid: stall at 7,466 band
Why this probability★★ 9% — V-shaped full reversals happen, but they need the catalyst itself to reverse (yields down), which isn't the base expectation into a 2-yr auction + Thursday PCE. Low odds, high reward if 7,463 reclaims.
G6 · Capitulation flush → 7,357/7,350SHORT then BUY★ 5%
Zone7,399 → 7,357 → 7,350
Narrative50-EMA breaks decisively (VIX > 22), forced selling carries to the 7,357 prev POC / 7,350 fib — the deep responsive-buy zone.
SetupNo chase; BUY 7,350–7,358 on a wick + reclaim, stop 7,338, target 7,399 / 7,449
Confirm / InvalidConfirm: VIX > 22 + 50-EMA closes below · Invalid: 7,399 holds
Why this probability★ 5% — requires a second leg of fear (VIX > 22) on top of an already-large move; possible only on a genuinely bad auction/data shock. Tail risk, sized accordingly.

05Probability Matrix — RTH

RankScenarioBiasProb
1G1 Bounce-to-sell 7,449–7,466Bear32%
2G2 Shelf-break continuationBear24%
3G3 Shelf-hold reclaim 7,463Bull18%
4G4 Open-drive-downBear12%
5G5 Snap-back to 7,533Bull9%
6G6 Capitulation → 7,357Bear5%
CombinedBear 73% · Bull 27%100%
Base case & reasoningG1 + G2 (sell rallies / sell breaks, 56% combined, Bear 73% overall). This is a deliberate inversion of yesterday's bull-73% plan, and the evidence forces it: (1) value migrated lower three sessions running and price is now cleanly below all of it; (2) the 20-EMA — the spring's buy line — was lost without a defense; (3) VIX is finally rising (+5.4%), so the "orderly, lean-less-bearish" modifier from Monday is gone; (4) the catalyst is structural (yields breakout + AI-capex), not a one-day rotation. The bull case (G3/G5, 27%) rests on the still-rising 50-EMA and the fact that the broader trend is intact — which is why longs are counter-trend fades at the 7,399–7,409 cluster, not the base case. What would flip this back to neutral/bull: a 30-min reclaim of 7,463 (refills the prints, flips broken value back to support) or the 2-yr auction stopping through with yields easing. Name either and the bear weighting falls.

06Decision Map — RTH

TUE OPEN ~7,436 (below 20-EMA, above 50-EMA)
│
├─ BOUNCES into 7,449–7,466 sell band
│    ├─ rejects (first tag + VIX bid) ──► SHORT ──► 7,422 / 7,409 / 7,399        [G1]
│    └─ accepts > 7,463 (30-min close) ──► flip ──► 7,496 / 7,514 / 7,533        [G3/G5]
│
├─ HOLDS 7,421 / 7,409 shelf
│    └─ reversal-up ──► reclaim 7,449 ──► test 7,463 ──► [see above]            [G3]
│
└─ BREAKS 7,409 (30-min close)
     ├─ 50-EMA 7,399 holds on a wick ──► responsive BUY scalp only
     └─ 50-EMA breaks / VIX > 22 ──► 7,357 prev POC ──► 7,350 fib BUY zone           [G2/G6]
LevelHolds →Breaks →
7,449–7,466 bandSell-the-rally works; back to lows7,496 → 7,514 → 7,533 short-cover squeeze
7,463 (reclaim)Bear intact below itFlips bias to two-sided/bull; prints refill
7,421 ON lowBounce base for G1/G37,409 shelf next

07Bias Flip Conditions — RTH

Start biasFlip toTriggerAction
Bear (sell rallies)Two-sided30m close > 7,463Stop shorting; prints refill, look long to 7,496/7,533
Two-sidedBullaccept > 7,496 spike baseLong the reclaim, target old value 7,533/7,554
BearStrong bear30m close < 7,399 50-EMAPress shorts to 7,357; trend-reversal context
Bull (shelf-hold)Bearreject < 7,440 after a bounceAbandon long; rejoin G1 shorts

#1
Bounce-to-sell short 7,449–7,463 (G1)
SHORTup to 3.1R▶

Short 7,449–7,463 on the FIRST tag of the broken ON VAL / prev VAL band with a rejection wick (let the bounce refill the thin prints up to you)
Stop
7,470 — above the band / single-print top · risk 8–14 pts
Targets
T1 7,422 ON lowT2 7,409 shelfT3 7,399 50-EMA

⅓ at ON low · ⅓ at shelf · runner to 50-EMA; trail above 7,449 once price is back < 7,422
R:R
1.4R / 2.4R / 3.1R
Kill switch
30-min close > 7,466 → prints refilling as support, exit; reassess for G3/G5 long
#2
Shelf-break continuation short 7,408 (G2)
SHORTup to 3.0R▶
Entry
Short 7,406–7,410 on a confirmed 30-min break of the 7,421 ON low + 7,409 shelf (retest-and-fail preferred over chasing)
Stop
7,425 — back above the shelf · risk 15–19 pts
Targets
T1 7,399 50-EMAT2 7,375T3 7,357 prev POC
Scaling
½ at 50-EMA (expect a bounce there) · hold runner only if 7,399 closes below; cover into 7,357
R:R
0.6R / 1.7R / 3.0R
Kill switch
Reclaim 7,422 → failed breakdown, flip to G3 long
#3
Shelf-hold counter-trend long 7,410–7,422 (G3)
LONGup to 3.4R▶
Entry
Long 7,410–7,422 at the prev VAH / 50-EMA cluster on a wick + reclaim — responsive buy at the trend line (counter-trend, smaller size)
Stop
7,398 — below the 50-EMA · risk 12–24 pts
Targets
T1 7,449 ON VALT2 7,463 (reclaim = add)T3 7,496
Scaling
½ at ON VAL (the prints fill fast); only hold past 7,463 if it closes above — that's the bias flip
R:R
1.1R / 1.7R / 3.4R
Kill switch
30-min close < 7,399 → 50-EMA lost, flatten and flip to G2/G6
Position size vs ATR — applies to all threeATR expanded to 110.25 and the ON range was ~128 pts — volatility regime is up, so use WIDER stops but SMALLER size (50–60% of normal) to keep $-risk constant. The shorts (G1/G2) are the trend-aligned, higher-conviction side; the long (G3) is a counter-trend fade — ½ size, take T1 quickly. Hard rule: it's a 2-yr auction + pre-PCE tape — flatten into the auction print and don't hold size through it. A 30-min reclaim of 7,463 is the line that invalidates the whole bear book.

09Structural Warnings — RTH

⚠ ON single prints 7,466 / 7,479 / 7,493 — upside speed zonesThin auction created on the way down. A bounce will travel through them FAST (no resistance inside), then hit the real wall at the broken value 7,449–7,463. Don't short inside the prints — wait for the band top; don't expect them to support price on the way back down either.
⚠ Broken spike base 7,496 + shelf 7,514 — now resistanceThese were support in yesterday's plan; once broken they flip to overhead supply. A deep bounce that reaches them should be sold unless reclaimed on a closing basis — old support becomes new resistance.
⚠ 50-EMA 7,399 — the trend lineThis is the level that separates "deep pullback" from "trend reversal." First test usually produces a bounce (hence G3), but a 30-min CLOSE below it changes the daily character and opens 7,357 / 7,300s. Don't be long under a broken 50-EMA.
⚠ Event risk — 2-yr auction + PCE ThursdayYields are the driver. A weak 2-yr auction today or a hot PCE Thursday is the gap-down fuel; flatten size into the auction. This is NOT a tape to hold through a binary catalyst.
⚠ Extension / snap-back risk (for shorts)Price is 97 pts below yesterday's POC — chasing shorts at the lows risks a violent short-cover snap-back into the thin prints. Sell the RALLY into 7,449–7,466, not the low.
ℹ VIX gate (inverted from yesterday)VIX < 19 = move may be exhausting, tighten shorts · 19–22 = trend-down active, sell rallies · > 22 = capitulation leg, stand aside and buy only the 7,357/7,350 deep shelf.

10Session Transition — Tue open zones

If price at open is…ImplicationLikely day type
> 7,496 (reclaimed spike base)Overnight flush rejected — short squeeze underwayReversal-up; long pullbacks to 7,463, target 7,533
7,463–7,496 (in broken band)Bounce filling prints, testing broken value from belowSell the band unless 7,463 closes above (then flip)
7,449–7,463 (ON VAL band)At first resistance, prints filledG1 sell-the-rally; primary short zone
7,421–7,449 (between edges)No-man's-land; open coilWait for the edge — fade 7,449, buy/short 7,421 break
7,399–7,421 (at the shelf)Testing support / 50-EMAG3 counter-trend long on a hold; G2 short on a close-below
< 7,399 (below 50-EMA)Trend line lost — character changeOpen-drive-down / trend day to 7,357; responsive longs only at 7,350

11Final Summary — Tue Jun 23 RTH

ItemCall
Macro biasBULL but tested — 50-EMA & full lower stack still rising; first serious trend test since April
Intermediate biasBEAR — broke down from balance; value lower 3 sessions, 20-EMA lost, VIX rising
Intraday biasSELL RALLIES into 7,449–7,466; respect 7,399–7,409 as the make-or-break support
Base caseOpen near the shelf → bounce to refill the ON single prints into 7,449–7,466 → rejection → back toward 7,409/7,399; a 7,399 close-below extends to 7,357
Primary tradeShort 7,449–7,463 first-tag rejection, stop 7,470, targets 7,422 / 7,409 / 7,399 (G1)
Best-location altShort 7,406–7,410 on a confirmed 7,409 shelf break, stop 7,425 → 7,399 / 7,357 (G2)
Counter-trend longLong 7,410–7,422 at the 50-EMA/shelf, stop 7,398 → 7,449 / 7,463 / 7,496 (G3) — ½ size, the only bull play, needs a 7,463 reclaim to extend
Key bull level7,463 reclaim (30-min close) — refills prints, flips broken value to support, opens 7,496/7,533
Key bear level7,399 (50-EMA) — a close below turns deep-pullback into trend-reversal; opens 7,357 → 7,350
Biggest riskA violent short-cover snap-back through the thin overnight prints traps chased shorts; and the 2-yr auction / PCE are binary yield catalysts
Day-type expectationBounce-to-sell (≈32%) · shelf-break continuation (≈24%) · shelf-hold reclaim (≈18%) · open-drive-down (≈12%) · snap-back (≈9%) · capitulation (≈5%)
ES RTH update for Tue Jun 23, 2026 · built 06:05 ET pre-open · re-anchored to the overnight yield/AI flush · ref 7,436.25 · ON low 7,421.75 · ON VAH 7,549.50 / ON POC 7,533.00 / ON VAL 7,449.00 · ON single prints 7,493.50 / 7,479.25 / 7,466.25 · broken: 7,514 shelf / 7,496 spike base / 7,463 prev VAL · support cluster 7,396–7,409 (50-EMA + prev VAH + fib) · 20-EMA 7,515.50 LOST · Catalyst: 10-yr ~4.51%, 2-yr 16-mo high (hawkish Warsh Fed) + AI-capex selloff · VIX 19.45 (+5.4%) · ATR 110.25 · Event risk: 2-yr auction + PMIs/ADP today, Micron Wed, PCE Thu · Not financial advice. Flatten size into the auction.
